plugin.name: WASM Web Widget
plugin.description: A second sandboxed WASM plugin that returns widget-oriented output.

capabilities.description: Returns markdown-like widget content from a sandboxed module.
Describes how the widget degrades across richer and simpler host surfaces.

actions.label: Render widget
actions.description: Return a widget-oriented response from a sandboxed module.

required.detail: Hosts must surface the structured widget payload.

optional.detail: Browser-style hosts can render cards instead of plain summaries.
optional.fallback: CLI hosts can print the widget body as markdown or JSON.

optional.detail: Hosts can label the WASM isolation boundary.
optional.fallback: Fallback hosts can still execute the module without isolation-specific chrome.

tested_hosts.notes: CLI hosts degrade rich UI to deterministic markdown-like output.
Web hosts can present richer cards while keeping the same output contract.

notes: The widget manifest is authored to stay forward-compatible with additive rendering metadata.
The module never touches the DOM or outbound network directly.
Hosts decide how to map the returned widget payload onto their own surfaces.

lifecycle.health_probe: Render a canned incident widget and verify that the markdown payload still parses.
notes: Suspend/resume metadata models UI hosts that cache widget state between frames.
The current runtime still instantiates the module per invocation.
Rich hosts may treat rendering as a background task while preserving the same contract.
Foreground CLI rendering remains valid when async job tracking is unavailable.

required.detail: Hosts need to surface the widget payload or forward it downstream.

optional.detail: Browser-oriented hosts can render richer visual cards.
optional.fallback: Other hosts can fall back to markdown-like widget text.

optional.detail: Hosts can label the WASM isolation boundary.
optional.fallback: If unavailable the host should omit sandbox-specific labels only.

optional.detail: Async-capable hosts can queue widget rendering jobs.
optional.fallback: The widget can still render synchronously for deterministic demos.

degradation.behavior: If rich-ui is unavailable the host should print the widget body as markdown or plain text.
If sandboxed-runtime is unavailable the host should omit isolation-specific badges.
If async-jobs is unavailable the host should render the widget in the foreground.

notes: This complements the generic wasm-sandboxed example with a UI-oriented degradation story.
This complements the generic wasm-sandboxed example with a more UI-flavored response.
Phase 4 uses it to demonstrate advanced WASM execution metadata and host-specific degradation.

release.installer_hint: Keep wasm-plugin.json and module.wat together so widget hosts can discover both the manifest and module.
